text: Stack/Queue Pattern: Monotonic stack (next greater element). Find next element greater than each element. Maintain stack of indices of elements awaiting their 'next greater'. For each element: pop all stack elements smaller than current — current is their next greater. Push current index. Time: O(n). Use for: daily temperatures, largest rectangle in histogram, next greater element.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Monotonic stack (previous smaller element). For each element, find nearest smaller element to the left. Maintain stack of elements/indices. Before pushing current, pop all elements >= current. Top of stack after popping = previous smaller element. Time: O(n). Combined with next-smaller: find the range where each element is the minimum — use in sum of subarray minimums.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Largest rectangle in histogram. For each bar, find how far it can extend (bounded by shorter bars on each side). Monotonic stack: push indices. When bar shorter than stack top found, pop and compute area (height=bars[popped], width=i-stack_top-1). Time: O(n). Apply to: maximal rectangle in binary matrix by computing histogram per row.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Valid parentheses. Use stack. Push opening brackets. On closing bracket: if stack empty or top doesn't match: invalid. Pop matching opening bracket. At end: stack must be empty. Time: O(n), Space: O(n). Variants: longest valid parentheses (stack of indices or DP), minimum add to make valid (count unmatched open and close).

Stack/Queue Pattern: Min stack (constant time minimum). Support push, pop, top, getMin in O(1). Maintain auxiliary min_stack. On push: push (val, min(val, current_min)) to min_stack. On pop: pop from both stacks. getMin: return min_stack top's min. Alternatively: only push to min_stack when new val <= current min (push old min on push, pop on pop of regular stack). Space: O(n).

Stack/Queue Pattern: Evaluate reverse Polish notation (RPN). Process tokens left to right. If number: push to stack. If operator: pop two operands, apply operator, push result. Result is stack's final element. Handles expressions in postfix notation without parentheses. Time: O(n). Use for: expression evaluation, compiler design, calculator implementations.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Decode string. Decode s like 3[a2[bc]] → abcbcabcbcabcbc. Stack stores (count, current_string) before each '['. On '[': push (k, current), reset k and current. On ']': (prev_k, prev_str) = pop; current = prev_str + prev_k * current. Digits: accumulate k. Letters: append to current. Time: O(output_length). Space: O(nesting_depth).

Stack/Queue Pattern: Daily temperatures (monotonic stack application). Find days until warmer temperature. Monotonic stack of indices (decreasing temperatures). When temp[i] > temp[stack.top()]: pop index j, result[j] = i-j. Push i. Time: O(n). General pattern: whenever stack top is 'resolved' by current element, pop and record answer. Process remaining stack as 'never resolved' (answer = 0).

Stack/Queue Pattern: BFS with queue (level order). Queue ensures FIFO processing, giving level-by-level (breadth-first) traversal. Deque (double-ended queue) is efficient: popleft() is O(1) vs list.pop(0) which is O(n). Use deque for BFS in Python: from collections import deque. Key: queue size at level start tells how many nodes are in current level.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Implement queue using stacks. Two stacks: inbox and outbox. Push: add to inbox. Pop/peek: if outbox empty, move all from inbox to outbox (reverses order), then pop from outbox. Amortized O(1) per operation (each element moved at most twice). Time: O(1) amortized. Demonstrates how two LIFO structures simulate FIFO.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Implement stack using queues. Two queues. Push: enqueue to q1. Pop: move all but last from q1 to q2, dequeue last element of q1, swap q1 and q2. Alternative: single queue — push to back, then rotate (move all but new element to back). Pop: just dequeue front. Push: O(n), Pop: O(1). Or Push: O(1), Pop: O(n).

Stack/Queue Pattern: Circular queue. Fixed-size queue using array. Pointers: front and rear. rear = (rear+1) % size on enqueue. front = (front+1) % size on dequeue. Empty: front==rear. Full: (rear+1)%size == front. More space-efficient than dynamic queue. Use for: bounded buffers, producer-consumer, sliding window problems with fixed window.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Monotonic deque (sliding window maximum). Maintain deque of indices in decreasing order of values. Add right element: pop from back while deque back is smaller. Remove left element: pop from front if it's the expired element. Front of deque = max in current window. Time: O(n). Generalizes: sliding window min/max in O(n) vs O(n log n) with heap.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Trap rainwater with stack. Process each bar. Maintain stack of indices (decreasing bars). When taller bar found: pop middle bar, compute water trapped between popped bar and current bar. Water height = min(height[stack.top()], height[current]) - height[popped]. Width = current - stack.top() - 1. Accumulate water. Time: O(n). Alternative: two-pointer O(n) O(1) space.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Asteroid collision. Stack simulates moving asteroids. Positive: moving right (push). Negative: moving left (may collide with top). While stack has positive top and current is negative: smaller absolute destroys, equal both destroy, negative smaller gets destroyed. If stack empty or top is negative: push current. Time: O(n).

Stack/Queue Pattern: Simplify Unix path. Process path components using stack. Split by '/'. Skip empty strings and '.'. For '..': pop stack if not empty. Otherwise: push component. Join with '/' and prepend '/'. Time: O(n). Stack naturally handles the 'go up one directory' operation. Similar pattern: evaluate nested operations, track context.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Basic calculator. Evaluate expression with +, -, (, ). Use stack to handle parentheses. When '(': push current result and sign to stack, reset. When ')': multiply by sign on stack (if -(expr)), add saved result. Track running result and sign. Handle multi-digit numbers. Time: O(n). Extension: include * and / with precedence — use two stacks or postfix conversion.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Remove K digits to form smallest number. Use monotonic stack. For each digit, pop stack while stack top is larger and k > 0. Push current digit. After processing, if k > 0: remove from end. Remove leading zeros. Return result. Time: O(n). Greedy insight: removing a larger digit from earlier position gives smaller number.

Stack/Queue Pattern: Sum of subarray minimums. For each element, find number of subarrays where it is the minimum. Use monotonic stack to find previous smaller (left boundary) and next smaller or equal (right boundary). Count = (i - left) * (right - i). Sum all element * count. Handle ties carefully to avoid double counting. Time: O(n). Stack-based O(n) vs brute force O(n²).

Stack/Queue Pattern: Priority queue vs regular queue vs stack. Stack: LIFO, DFS, backtracking, undo operations. Queue: FIFO, BFS, level order, scheduling. Priority queue (heap): highest-priority element first, Dijkstra, greedy scheduling, streaming top-K. Deque: both ends, sliding window, BFS with early termination. Choose based on access pattern: last-in (stack), first-in (queue), best-first (priority queue).
